Title: Biosynthesis and bioactivity of antiinflammatory triterpenoids in Calendula officinalis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of faradiol palmitate when co-expressed with upstream pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly floral; upregulated in ray florets compared with disc florets; highest in young buds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate gene co-expression with CoTXSS and CoCYP716A392 in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoACT1 encodes a triterpene acyltransferase involved in downstream acylation of faradiol. 2. Co-expression with CoTXSS and CoCYP716A392 increased faradiol palmitate above endogenous background in N. benthamiana. 3. CoACT1 is flower-enriched and likely contributes to faradiol fatty acid ester biosynthesis in pot marigold.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely homeologue of CoACT2; did not increase taraxasterol/Ψ-taraxasterol palmitate when expressed with CoTXSS alone.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing or engineering faradiol fatty acid ester production together with CoTXSS and CoCYP716A392/393.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly GCA_964273985.1; transcriptomes and differential expression data at Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-inflammatory bioactive triterpenoid ester contributing to pot marigold floral extract activity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of faradiol palmitate when co-expressed with upstream pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly floral; upregulated in ray florets compared with disc florets; highest in young buds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate gene co-expression with CoTXSS and CoCYP716A392 in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoACT2 functions as a triterpene acyltransferase for faradiol palmitate formation. 2. Together with CoTXSS and CoCYP716A392, it increased faradiol palmitate accumulation in N. benthamiana. 3. CoACT2 is co-expressed in floral tissues and likely acts as a homeologous partner to CoACT1.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely homeologue of CoACT1; genes are in conserved syntenic regions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful module for reconstituting medicinal triterpene ester biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly GCA_964273985.1; transcriptomes and differential expression data at Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-inflammatory bioactive triterpenoid ester contributing to pot marigold floral extract activity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases palmitate esterification of taraxasterol/Ψ-taraxasterol scaffolds.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More highly expressed in flowers than leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with CoTXSS in N. benthamiana and GC-MS product profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoACT3 acylates taraxasterol/Ψ-taraxasterol scaffolds. 2. Among tested CoACTs, CoACT3 produced the highest quantities of Ψ-taraxasterol/taraxasterol palmitate. 3. It is implicated in scaffold esterification but not the major faradiol palmitate-forming step.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active on triterpene scaffolds when CoTXSS is expressed alone; not the principal faradiol acyltransferase.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to engineer scaffold ester derivatives and broaden triterpene ester product diversity.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly GCA_964273985.1; transcriptomes and differential expression data at Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized triterpene ester; bioactivity not established here as strongly as faradiol esters

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pentacyclic triterpenoid/faradiol fatty acid ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Ψ-taraxasterol and taraxasterol; trace β-amyrin and lupeol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to primarily Ψ-taraxasterol and taraxasterol pentacyclic triterpene scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms core scaffold precursor for faradiol and faradiol fatty acid esters
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step; key branch-defining scaffold-forming enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of Ψ-taraxasterol-derived anti-inflammatory triterpenoids and determines scaffold specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly floral; highest in young buds; one homeolog expressed while another candidate copy is not expressed/pseudogenized
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana with co-expression of tHMGR and P19
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome/transcriptome mining, phylogenetics, transient expression in N. benthamiana, GC-MS metabolite profiling, mutagenesis, structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoTXSS was identified as the pot marigold oxidosqualene cyclase producing mainly Ψ-taraxasterol/taraxasterol scaffolds. 2. It catalyzes the first committed step toward faradiol and faradiol esters. 3. Mutagenesis and phylogeny support that TXSS evolved from a multifunctional amyrin synthase and specific residues govern product specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produces more Ψ-taraxasterol than taraxasterol; key residues include I367 and E371 versus MAS, and D385/H492/G380/P751 affect Ψ-taraxasterol:taraxasterol ratio.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central entry enzyme for reconstructing anti-inflammatory triterpenoid biosynthesis in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome GCA_964273985.1; reads PRJEB80524; transcriptome/differential expression Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Faradiol and its esters are anti-inflammatory and suppress IL-6 release; faradiol reduces STAT3 phosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multifunctionality also yields minor side-products β-amyrin and lupeol
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: tHMGR; P19; CoCYP716A392; CoCYP716A393; CoACT1; CoACT2; CoACT3

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C16β hydroxylation of Ψ-taraxasterol to form faradiol and also hydroxylates β-amyrin and lupeol to corresponding diols.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the bioactive intermediate faradiol from the triterpene scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream tailoring step for anti-inflammatory C16-hydroxylated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of faradiol, a major anti-inflammatory triterpene diol precursor to faradiol esters.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly floral; highest in mature buds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana; endogenous metabolism may derivatize products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetics, floral expression analysis, co-expression with CoTXSS in N. benthamiana, GC-MS metabolite profiling, mutagenesis, structural modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoCYP716A392 is a C16β-hydroxylase that converts Ψ-taraxasterol to faradiol. 2. It also accepts β-amyrin and lupeol, generating maniladiol and calenduladiol. 3. Together with CoTXSS and CoACT1/2, it enables reconstruction of faradiol palmitate biosynthesis in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely homeologue of CoCYP716A393; mutations A285V, A357L, and H424R shift activity toward β-amyrin relative to Ψ-taraxasterol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential tailoring enzyme for engineering anti-inflammatory faradiol production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome GCA_964273985.1; reads PRJEB80524; transcriptome/differential expression Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Faradiol strongly inhibits LPS-induced IL-6 release and reduces STAT3 phosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity may divert flux to other diols; product recovery confounded by endogenous N. benthamiana metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C16β hydroxylation of Ψ-taraxasterol to faradiol and also acts on β-amyrin and lupeol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms faradiol intermediate for faradiol ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of bioactive C16-hydroxylated triterpenes including faradiol.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly floral; highest in mature buds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with CoTXSS in N. benthamiana and GC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetics and expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CoCYP716A393 is a homeologous C16β-hydroxylase functionally similar to CoCYP716A392. 2. It converts Ψ-taraxasterol to faradiol and also acts on β-amyrin and lupeol. 3. It contributes to the anti-inflammatory triterpenoid pathway in flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 83.69% identical to CoCYP716A392 and located in conserved syntenic regions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative or complementary hydroxylase for pathway reconstitution and flux optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome GCA_964273985.1; reads PRJEB80524; transcriptome/differential expression Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.13869958
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Faradiol is anti-inflammatory and suppresses IL-6 release
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity may generate side diols
